title: Displays, Cameras, Audio, and Accessible Output
description: A hybrid classroom has unreadable text, intermittent external video, echo, camera framing failures, color-only status, and conflicting accessibility and privacy needs. Learners cumulatively produce accessible audiovisual signal-path diagnosis through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare LCD panel types, OLED, projectors, digitizers, backlights, inverters, touch layers, privacy filters, and failure signatures.
Relate physical size, pixel resolution, density, scaling, refresh, orientation, reflow, zoom, and readable workspace to the user task.
Trace source, GPU, port, cable, adapter, dock, sink, input selection, negotiation, power, and operating-system state before replacing parts.
